svg.aria-label: The two data contracts: ingestion and artifact
svg.text: The two data contracts
One at the way in, one at the way out. Both are enforced, and a violation fails loudly rather than propagating quietly.
CONTRACT 1, INGESTION
raw bytes to a validated table
Required schema, units, ranges, and an EXPLICIT outlier and missing-value policy. Bad data is rejected, never silently coerced.
Fatal violations raise. Warnings are recorded in the manifest, so a reader sees what the pipeline noticed and chose to allow.
Generic target-broadcast detector: if the target takes far fewer
distinct values than there are rows, fitting at that resolution leaks it.
Real defects carried verbatim: one source states no missing values
and ships 591; one recorded URL served an entirely different dataset.
CONTRACT 2, ARTIFACT
pipeline to web, frozen at schema_version 1.0.0
A TypeScript type mirrors this schema, so a drift between what Python writes and what the app expects fails the BUILD.
Every float rounded before it ships.
Node ids are ONE integer space, shared by
the equation, the tree and the terms.
Colour index assigned in Python, so the
equation, tree and contribution palettes
cannot drift apart.
Every downsample discloses its original
count. Showing part of the data is fine;
not saying so is not.
